summary: PR 2652 is a one-line tooling/documentation fix in `tools/wiki_tu.py`: `ColumnIndex.FILE` was changed from `auto()` to explicit `0`, leaving later enum members as `auto()`.

key_files.role: Changed tooling script defining `ColumnIndex`; first enum value changed to explicit zero.

what_changed.text: `ColumnIndex.FILE` changed from `auto()` to `0`; `MATCHED`, `TOTAL`, and `REMAINING` remain assigned with `auto()`.
The PR changed one file with one added line and one deleted line.

smart_moves.text: The fix was narrowly scoped to the enum member that needs zero-based assignment, avoiding unrelated changes to the wiki tooling script.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For Python tooling enums that represent table or list column indexes, confirm whether the first column must be zero-based before using `auto()`; Python enum `auto()` does not express an explicit zero starting value in this diff.
evidence_refs: PR title: Fix column enum assignment in `wiki_tu.py`

follow_up_queries: ColumnIndex IntEnum auto FILE MATCHED TOTAL REMAINING

notes: The diff is complete and very small, so changed-file and code-change evidence is strong.
There are no PR body details, issue comments, review comments, reviews, or validation output in the slice.
No reviewer intent, test result, runtime failure mode, or downstream wiki output behavior is evidenced beyond the title and one-line diff.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use explicit zero for zero-based column index IntEnums
accepted_candidate_records.text: In `tools/wiki_tu.py`, `ColumnIndex.FILE` was fixed from `auto()` to `0`, while later column enum entries continued using `auto()`. For tooling enums that map directly to zero-based column positions, verify the starting value instead of relying on `auto()` for the first member.
